requiredWorkflows: Assistant chat and playground make a real model call instead of returning API key not configured.
Repository code browser and clone URLs use real Gitea data rather than placeholder/template data.
Agent dispatch reaches Agent Mux and produces job/session/transcript evidence.
verificationCommands: helm template krate packages/krate/charts -n krate-system --set argocd.enabled=false

helm template krate packages/krate/charts -n krate-system --set argocd.enabled=false --set gitea.httpUrl=http://gitea-http.krate-system.svc.cluster.local:3000/krate --set agentMux.url=http://agent-mux.krate-system.svc.cluster.local:8080 --set assistant.existingSecret=krate-assistant --set gitea.token.existingSecret=krate-gitea-token
secretExposureChecks: No plaintext Anthropic API key or Gitea token appears in source, rendered manifests, logs, PR body, or issue comments.
Rendered manifests use secretKeyRef or other approved secret references for token-bearing values.
Agent and verification output redacts secret values and reports only object/key names.
smokeChecks: GET https://krate-staging.a5c.ai/api/orgs/default/snapshot reports Kubernetes, Gitea, and Agent Mux healthy.
Assistant chat/playground returns a model response and usage metadata without API key errors.
Repository tree/blob routes return source:gitea for a real repository.
Dispatch creates a real Agent Mux-backed job/session/transcript path.
